title: Cron plugin with a dynamic-looking schedule (but actually static)
prompt: A common LLM mistake: treating `Oban.Plugins.Cron` as if it supports dynamic/database-driven crontabs. It doesn't — the free plugin loads the crontab STATICALLY at boot time. `Oban.Pro.DynamicCron` is the only dynamic option. Write a `MyApp.CronSchedule` module that exposes a `@crontab` module attribute with three entries. Document explicitly in the `@moduledoc` that `Oban.Plugins.Cron` loads at boot time only, runtime updates require a supervisor restart or Oban Pro's DynamicCron. Also include a function `MyApp.CronSchedule.plugin_config/0` that returns `{Oban.Plugins.Cron, [crontab: @crontab]}` for use in the runtime config. The `@crontab` should contain three distinct entries. Write to `lib/my_app/cron_schedule.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the difference between free and Pro cron plugins and documenting the limitation. Pre-training-data LLMs reach for dynamic solutions first.
research_citation: Oban.Plugins.Cron HexDocs — 'This plugin only loads the crontab statically, at boot time.'
